zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Dugald Duncan Campbell IInd of Craignish於1182年出生在英國蘇格蘭阿吉爾洛霍時，他的父親Sir Archibald Campbell24歲，母親Anna McDonald0歲。他於1198年與Findoig Macgillivrail在蘇格蘭阿吉爾洛霍結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於大約1220年在英國蘇格蘭享年39歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭阿吉爾洛霍。

nameMeanings.name: Campbell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Camble
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Campbell  .

variants.name: Campbel
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Campbell  .

variants.name: Cambell
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Campbell  .

variants.name: Campell
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire) and English: variant of   Campbell   (see also   Camel  ).

variants.name: Beauchamp
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Camel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Napoleon, Patrice.

English (of Norman origin) and French: nickname from Norman French came(i)l ‘camel’ (from Latin camelus, classical Greek kamēlos), possibly denoting a clumsy person or someone with long legs. It may also be a topographic or habitational name referring to a house with a sign depicting a camel (although surnames derived from house and inn signs are rare in English); compare 5 below.
English: possibly a habitational name from Queen Camel and West Camel in Somerset, early recorded as Camel(le), possibly a Celtic name from canto- ‘border, district’ and mēl ‘bare hill’.

nameMeanings.name: Dugald
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Anglicized form of Dubhghall . The final -d is due to a mishearing of the Gaelic form, in which the final -ll sounds like -ld to English ears.

variants.name: Dougal
meanings: Anglicized form of   Dubhghall .

variants.name: Dubhghall
meanings: Gaelic: from dubh ‘black, dark’ + gall ‘stranger’. This was a byname applied to Danes, in contrast to the fairer Norwegians and Icelanders ( compare Fionnghall ).

nameMeanings.name: Duncan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Anglicized form of the Gaelic name Donnchadh , also used among people without Scottish connections. This was the name of a 7th-century Scottish saint (abbot of Iona) and two medieval kings of Scotland. The final ‐n in the Anglicized form seems to be the result of confusion with the Gaelic word ceann ‘head’ in the Latinized form Duncanus. Compare Irish Donagh .

variants.name: Donnchadh
meanings: Gaelic: compound of Old Celtic donn ‘brown’ + chadh ‘chief’ or ‘noble’.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Dugald Duncan Campbell IInd of Craignish was born in 1182, in Lochawe, Argyll, Scotland, United Kingdom, his father, Sir Archibald Campbell, was 24 and his mother, Anna McDonald, was 0. He married Findoig Macgillivrail in 1198, in Lochawe, Argyll, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 1 daughter. He died about 1220, in Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 39, and was buried in Lochawe, Argyll, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Dugald Duncan Campbell IInd of Craignish est né en 1182, à Lochawe, Argyll, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Archibald Campbell, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Anna McDonald, avait 0 ans. Il a épousé Findoig Macgillivrail en 1198, à Lochawe, Argyll, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé environ 1220, à Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 39 ans, et a été enterré à Lochawe, Argyll, Écosse.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Dugald Duncan Campbell IInd of Craignishが1182年にイギリススコットランドアルグイルロチャウで生まれた時，父Sir Archibald Campbellは24歳，母Anna McDonaldは0歳であった。1198年にスコットランドアルグイルロチャウでFindoig Macgillivrailと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は約1220年にイギリススコットランドで39歳で死去し，スコットランドアルグイルロチャウにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Dugald Duncan Campbell IInd of Craignish(이)가 1182년 영국 스코틀랜드 아그이 럭아베에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Archibald Campbell(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Anna McDonald(은)는 0세였다. 그는 1198년 스컷랜드 아그이 럭아베에서 Findoig Macgillivrail(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1220년 영국 스코틀랜드에서 39세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 아그이 럭아베에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Dugald Duncan Campbell IInd of Craignish родился в 1182 г. в местности лочав, аргилл, Шотландия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir Archibald Campbell было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Anna McDonald, 0. Он женился на женщине по имени Findoig Macgillivrail в 1198 г. в местности лочав, аргилл, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 1 дочь. Он умер около 1220 г. в местности Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 39 лет и был похоронен в местности лочав, аргилл, Шотландия.
